established.categorical_necessity: any operation-capturing category C must include all six morphism-data pieces of TtS
established.categorical_sufficiency: a category with exactly the six morphism-data pieces captures the operation
established.categorical_minimality: no proper subset of the six pieces captures the operation; each independently witnessed
established.equivalence_theorem: any category satisfying necessity + sufficiency + minimality is categorically equivalent to TtS via the data-preserving functor F: TtS -> C (faithful + full + essentially surjective)
established.deductive_completeness: v35.4 categorical-formalization assumption closed by uniqueness theorem; gate framework is now deductively complete modulo A1 itself

open.status_determination_uniqueness: lemma that each gate-availability pattern leads to exactly one named status; empirically nine-for-nine, not deductively proved
open.A1_conditionality: gate framework conditional on A1; different foundational axiom would produce different category and gates (feature, not bug)

joint_state_after_v35_5.necessity: v35.2 — per-gate deductive proofs from A1 + MD + BW + categorical structure
joint_state_after_v35_5.sufficiency_by_enumeration: v35.3 — six surfaces enumerated; eleven candidate G7s reduce
joint_state_after_v35_5.sufficiency_by_deduction: v35.4 — morphism-data exhaustion in TtS; A1 forces gates by data correspondence
joint_state_after_v35_5.categorical_uniqueness: v35.5 — TtS unique up to categorical equivalence among operation-capturing categories

deductive_completeness_claim: For any A1-admissible operation-capturing category C, C is equivalent to TtS, and admissible exports in C are exactly those whose morphism data closes G1-G6
